title: I am the woman who has it. Not someday — now
subtitle: An affirmation for claiming financial abundance as a present identity, not a future goal.
excerpt: A short affirmation for Day 16 of the MONEY program. The statement claims the identity of the woman who has financial abundance in the present tense, bypassing the habit of deferring it to a future self.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: An affirmation for claiming the identity of the woman who has financial abundance, present tense. Not a future goal: the person you already are in the process of becoming. The title phrase "not someday. Now" is the specific correction.

The word "now" is doing the specific work here. Deferring identity to the future self is the most common way the exclusion belief survives affirmation practice. "Someday I'll be that woman" keeps the identity at arm's length. "Now" puts you inside it.
I am the woman who has it. Not someday. Now.
Say it three times in the morning, hand on heart. Let the "now" land each time. If resistance comes up, notice it and say the affirmation again. If a phrase doesn't feel like you, change it.
Adapted from Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
